Feature_Name: Inter-gNB-DU LTM Handover UE Context Setup
Feature_Description: This procedure is used for the case when the UE moves from one gNB-DU to another gNB-DU within the same gNB-CU during NR operation for LTM (LTM Handover).

The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell, containing one candidate cell ID and the CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM. The gNB-CU may provide the LTM configuration ID mapping list to the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request PRACH resources from the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request the candidate gNB-DU(s) to provide the lower layer configuration for the purpose of generating the reference configuration or provide the lower layer part of the reference configuration to the candidate gNB-DU(s).

If the candidate gNB-DU accepts the request of LTM configuration, it responds with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message including the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell.
Feature_Objectives: To create a UE context in the candidate gNB-DU for LTM Handover.
To setup one or more data bearers in the candidate gNB-DU.
To configure CSI resources for subsequent LTM.
To provide LTM configuration ID mapping list to the candidate gNB-DU.
To request PRACH resources from the candidate gNB-DU.
To exchange lower layer configuration for reference configuration generation.
Feature_Implementation_Steps: The gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration (Step 2 in Figure 8.2.1.5-1).

The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell, containing one candidate cell ID and the CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM. The gNB-CU may provide the LTM configuration ID mapping list to the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request PRACH resources from the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request the candidate gNB-DU(s) to provide the lower layer configuration for the purpose of generating the reference configuration or provide the lower layer part of the reference configuration to the candidate gNB-DU(s) (Step 3 in Figure 8.2.1.5-1).

If the candidate gNB-DU accepts the request of LTM configuration, it responds with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message including the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell (Step 4 in Figure 8.2.1.5-1).
Procedure_Implementation_Steps: 3. The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell, containing one candidate cell ID and the CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM. The gNB-CU may provide the LTM configuration ID mapping list to the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request PRACH resources from the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request the candidate gNB-DU(s) to provide the lower layer configuration for the purpose of generating the reference configuration or provide the lower layer part of the reference configuration to the candidate gNB-DU(s).

4. If the candidate gNB-DU accepts the request of LTM configuration, it responds with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message including the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell.

Code_Instructions.description: Data structure defining the format of the F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message, including information elements like UE-associated F1AP ID, gNB-CU UE F1AP ID, gNB-DU UE F1AP ID, RRC Container, and other context information needed for setup.

Data structure defining the format of the F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message, including information elements like UE-associated F1AP ID, gNB-CU UE F1AP ID, gNB-DU UE F1AP ID, and the generated lower layer RRC configurations.

This function likely handles the encoding of the F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message on the gNB-DU side before sending it to the gNB-CU, or decodes it on the gNB-CU side upon reception. It would involve populating the 'f1ap_ue_context_setup_resp_s' structure with relevant data.

This function likely handles the decoding of the F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message on the gNB-DU side upon reception from the gNB-CU. It would parse the received message and extract information into the 'f1ap_ue_context_setup_req_s' structure.
Helper function responsible for generating and populating the content of the F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message on the gNB-CU side, based on the UE's context and LTM configuration requirements.
A generic utility function to copy a byte array from a source to a destination. Useful for handling various Information Elements (IEs) within F1AP messages that are represented as byte arrays.

A utility function for memory allocation that ensures the allocation is successful, typically exiting or asserting on failure. Essential for dynamically allocating F1AP message structures or their internal components.
A utility function for memory allocation that initializes the allocated memory to zero and ensures the allocation is successful. Useful for F1AP message structures to ensure default values.
Utility function on the gNB-CU to retrieve UE-specific F1AP context data. This data is crucial for populating the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message and for managing the UE's state during the handover.
Utility function to generate a unique transaction identifier for F1AP messages. This is essential for correlating F1AP requests and responses, such as the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST and RESPONSE.
A generic utility function to access an element at a specific index within a sequence array. This can be useful for handling F1AP Information Elements that are structured as lists or sequences.

Piggy_Backed_IES_Info: Not applicable for the scoped messages in this procedure.
